testCases.title: Login - Token Generation
testCases.notes: Login with qa-agent-slot2@virtocommerce.com succeeded. POST /connect/token returned HTTP 200. Auth stored in localStorage with access_token (Bearer, JWT) and refresh_token. User redirected to home. 0 console errors.

testCases.title: Login - Token Persistence
testCases.notes: Navigated to /account/dashboard after login. Pressed F5 to reload. Page remained on /account/dashboard without redirect to /sign-in. Session persisted via HttpOnly cookie + localStorage token. 0 console errors.

testCases.title: Logout - Token Revocation
testCases.notes: Clicked Logout from account menu. POST /revoke/token returned HTTP 200. localStorage auth.access_token set to null after revoke. User returned to anonymous context. 0 console errors. Note: direct 'old token rejected by API' sub-check not independently tested in this case (covered by AUTH-057 attempt).

testCases.title: Impersonation - View as Customer
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Admin SPA (AngularJS 3.1022.0) navigation items consistently fail browser_click stability checks with 'waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable' 5s timeouts. Real-user click via Playwright Firefox MCP cannot reliably interact with Contacts side menu or contact-detail 'Login on behalf' control. Impersonation feature itself not evaluated — this is a tool+platform interaction constraint, not a product defect. Same pattern as SMK-013/014 iframe constraint in suite 042.

testCases.title: Impersonation - Switch Companies
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Depends on AUTH-043 impersonation initiation; Admin SPA click stability timeouts prevent entering impersonation session. Test should migrate to Chrome MCP where admin SPA interaction has been shown to be more reliable.

testCases.title: Impersonation - Stop Impersonation
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Depends on AUTH-043 impersonation initiation; Admin SPA AngularJS click stability timeouts prevent initiating impersonation.

testCases.title: Impersonation - Place Order on Behalf
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Depends on AUTH-043 impersonation initiation + CyberSource Microform v2 payment entry (cross-origin iframe constraint per SMK-013). Double-blocked.

testCases.title: Impersonation - No Persistent Side Effects
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Depends on AUTH-043 impersonation initiation; Admin SPA AngularJS click stability timeouts prevent initiating impersonation.

testCases.title: Session Expiry During Checkout - Cart Preserved After Re-Auth
testCases.notes: Logged in as qa-agent-slot2 with 6 items in cart. Simulated session expiry per CSV step ('Clear all cookies and local storage via browser console to simulate session expiry'). On reload, site reverted to anonymous state (Sign in/Sign up links visible, anonymous empty cart). Re-authenticated via sign-in form. Navigated to /cart — cart badge still shows 6 Cart items, authed cart fully restored server-side. Checkout is resumable. 0 console errors. POST /connect/token returned 200 on re-auth.

testCases.title: Concurrent Sessions - Both Active Independently
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: True concurrent-session isolation testing requires two separate Playwright browser contexts (Browser A + Browser B). The single playwright-firefox MCP server exposes only one browser context; multiple tabs within the same context share cookies/session so cannot simulate independent concurrent sessions. Test should migrate to multi-context orchestration or be restructured as 'same-user login in two isolated contexts' using playwright-chrome + playwright-edge in parallel.

testCases.title: Remember Me - Session Persistence Across Browser Restart
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Test requires closing browser and reopening in SAME context/profile to verify persistent cookie. Playwright MCP browser_close terminates the browser context entirely (isolated: true in mcp-playwright-firefox.config.json), invalidating the semantics of 'browser restart with persisted state'. Verified 'Remember me' checkbox IS visible and present on the sign-in form (e165 in snapshot). Persistent-cookie behavior test requires a non-isolated context runner.

testCases.title: Remember Me - Session Does Not Persist Without Checkbox
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Same constraint as AUTH-050 — MCP browser_close destroys context; cannot validate 'session-only cookie cleared on browser close' vs 'persistent cookie kept' distinction. Confirmed 'Remember me' checkbox is unchecked by default on sign-in form.

testCases.title: Token Replay After Logout - API Rejection
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: The logout action triggers full-page navigation on the storefront, wiping any window-scope or sessionStorage token holder used to preserve the captured bearer token across the logout. Cannot execute the 'old token rejected with 401' replay fetch within a single browser session. Partial verification: POST /revoke/token confirmed to return HTTP 200 on logout (see AUTH-024) — revocation IS triggered. Full token-replay verification requires HAR-based out-of-band capture or Postman MCP.

testCases.title: RBAC - Customer Cannot Access Admin API
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Browser CORS preflight blocks cross-origin fetch from storefront domain (vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com) to backend admin API (vcst-qa.govirto.com/api/platform/*). All 3 endpoints (/api/platform/settings, /api/platform/security/users, /api/platform/modules) returned 'NetworkError when attempting to fetch resource' — CORS correctly restricts customer JS from reaching admin APIs (defense in depth). Server-side RBAC rejection (401/403) cannot be directly observed from browser context; test should migrate to Postman MCP or backend API suite.

testCases.title: RBAC - Anonymous Cannot Access Authenticated Endpoints
testCases.notes: Anonymous POST /graphql with query '{ me { id userName memberId userType } }' returned HTTP 200 with data { userName: 'Anonymous', memberId: null, userType: null } — no authenticated user data exposed. Anonymous fetch to https://vcst-qa.govirto.com/api/platform/settings CORS-blocked from storefront domain (correct defense-in-depth). 0 console errors in test context. BL-AUTH-005 satisfied at xAPI layer. Note: full REST-side 401 check requires Postman MCP.

testCases.title: Protected Route Redirect - Unauthenticated Access to Account Pages
testCases.notes: Verified without active session: /account → /sign-in?returnUrl=/account/dashboard; /account/orders → /sign-in?returnUrl=/account/orders; /company/members → /sign-in?returnUrl=/company/members. All redirects preserve returnUrl. /checkout → /cart (cart-gated rather than auth-gated — anonymous carts exist prior to checkout; this is acceptable behavior). 0 console errors. Login form visible on sign-in pages. BL-AUTH-006 satisfied for account-scoped routes.

testCases.title: Protected Route Redirect - Return to Original Page After Login
testCases.notes: Navigated anonymously to /account/orders → redirected to /sign-in?returnUrl=/account/orders (returnUrl parameter confirmed in URL). After login via sign-in form with Enter key submit, user redirected directly to /account/orders (NOT home or dashboard). Page title QA & Orders. POST /connect/token returned 200. Return URL preserved and honored through authentication flow. 0 console errors.

testCases.title: Impersonation - Switch Companies While Impersonating
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Depends on impersonation initiation from Admin SPA; AngularJS click stability timeouts prevent test execution. Same root cause as AUTH-043/044.

testCases.title: Impersonation - Stop Impersonation Returns to Admin State
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Depends on impersonation initiation from Admin SPA; AngularJS click stability timeouts prevent test execution.

testCases.title: Impersonation - Banner Visible Throughout Browsing
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Depends on impersonation initiation from Admin SPA; AngularJS click stability timeouts prevent test execution.

testCases.title: Impersonation - Impersonated User Cannot Impersonate Others
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Depends on impersonation initiation from Admin SPA; AngularJS click stability timeouts prevent test execution. Privilege-escalation-via-nested-impersonation test requires the parent impersonation session to succeed first.

errors: AUTH-043 through AUTH-047, AUTH-062 through AUTH-065 (8 impersonation tests): Admin SPA (AngularJS 3.1022.0) nav items and grid rows fail Playwright browser_click stability checks with 5s 'waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable' timeouts. Real-user click cannot interact with Contacts menu, contact-detail panel, or 'Login on behalf' control in Firefox MCP. Not a product defect — a browser-automation interaction constraint. Consider Chrome MCP which has historically handled AngularJS admin SPA more reliably, or use dedicated Chrome DevTools MCP for admin-SPA-driven tests.
AUTH-049 (Concurrent Sessions): Requires two separate Playwright browser contexts. Single playwright-firefox MCP exposes only one context; tabs share cookies.
AUTH-050/AUTH-051 (Remember Me): Requires browser close + reopen with same context. MCP browser_close destroys isolated context (isolated:true in config), invalidating persistence test semantics.

AUTH-057 (Token Replay): Logout navigation wipes window-scope token holder; cannot preserve captured bearer token across logout to execute replay fetch. /revoke/token -> HTTP 200 verified (see AUTH-024).
AUTH-058 (Customer token on admin API): Browser CORS preflight blocks cross-origin fetch from storefront domain to backend /api/platform/*. Migrate to Postman MCP for server-side RBAC verification.
